campus: Milan (with field activities at the Maldives National University campus under the joint-degree partnership)
degree_class: LM-75 (Scienze e Tecnologie per l'Ambiente e il Territorio)
academic_requirements: Bachelor's degree (or equivalent foreign qualification) in a relevant scientific field. The programme requires curricular preparation in biology, chemistry, physics and mathematics measured in CFU/ECTS across the scientific-disciplinary sectors typical for L-13 / L-32 / L-30 / L-34 classes. Admission is 'open with entry requirements examination' — curricular requirements are verified first, then personal preparation is assessed.
language_requirements: Minimum B2 level of English is required for admission (the official language of the Master's Degree course is English). Accepted certifications include IELTS, TOEFL (e.g. TOEFL ITP: L+R+W = 543 or higher AND S = 58 or higher), Cambridge English and Duolingo English Test, plus other UNIMIB-accredited B2 certifications, the UNIMIB 'English B2' Open Badge, or a prior degree taught entirely or mainly in English. Native English speakers are exempt. Non-Italian students must additionally reach Italian B1 (UNIMIB provides the assessment free of charge).
entry_exam_or_test: Entry requirements examination — the admission board verifies curricular CFU/ECTS coverage in biology, chemistry, physics and mathematics and may carry out an interview to assess adequacy of personal preparation. No external standardised test (TOLC/GRE) is required.
application_deadline_eu: Unified UNIMIB Master's calls for 2026/2027 (EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy), per apply.unimib.it: 1st CALL: 10 February 2026 - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL: 15 April 2026 - 6 July 2026 (EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy); 3rd CALL: 7 July 2026 - 7 September 2026 (EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy). Italian-qualification candidates apply via Segreterie OnLine; foreign-qualification candidates apply via apply.unimib.it (Dream Apply).
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU citizens requiring a student visa must apply via apply.unimib.it within the earlier Master calls only: 1st CALL: 10 February 2026 - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL: 15 April 2026 - 6 July 2026. The 3rd CALL (7 July - 7 September 2026) is reserved for EU citizens and non-EU citizens already residing in Italy. English language certification must be submitted by 31 July 2026. Arrival in Italy for visa applicants by 31 January 2027; verification of original documents by 28 February 2027.
required_documents: Identity document (ID card or passport); digital ID photo (min 300x400 px, light background, candidate's face clearly visible); bachelor's degree certificate; academic transcript of records with grades and CFU/ECTS per subject area (biology, chemistry, physics, mathematics); English language certification at B2 level (IELTS, TOEFL — e.g. TOEFL ITP L+R+W ≥ 543 and S ≥ 58 — Cambridge, Duolingo) — exempt for native English speakers and graduates of fully English-taught programmes; CV; motivation letter; recommendation letters (typically 2–3); for non-EU applicants: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement; passport copy for visa applicants.

source_quotes.quote: The official language of the Master's Degree course is English (B2 level required for admission).
The program provides theoretical knowledge and practical experience, and entails 12 exams (120 credits).
Accepted institutions that issue competency certificates of no less than B2 level include TOEFL ITP (Score: L. R. W.: 543 or higher + S: 58 or higher).
Applicants must submit: Curriculum Vitae; Academic transcript; Motivation letter; Recommendation letters (typically 2-3).
1st CALL: November 7th, 2025 / January 16th, 2026; 2nd CALL: March 16th, 2026 / April 16th, 2026; 3rd CALL: May 15th, 2026 / June 15th, 2026; 4th CALL: July 7th, 2026 / September 7th, 2026.

uncertainty_notes: Exact CFU thresholds per subject area (biology/chemistry/physics/maths) are not numerically published on the public 2026/27 page — UNIMIB describes them qualitatively and verifies them case-by-case. Equivalent IELTS/TOEFL numeric thresholds beyond TOEFL ITP (543 / 58) are not listed individually — UNIMIB refers to its central accredited-certifications list. Deadlines come from the unified apply.unimib.it 2026/27 announcement; the dedicated Marine Sciences 2026/27 PDF is not yet published (current PDF is 2025/26). Deadlines re-verified 2026-06-11 against apply.unimib.it 2026/27 calendar (Master track: 1st CALL 10 Feb - 31 Mar 2026; 2nd CALL 15 Apr - 6 Jul 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]; 3rd CALL 7 Jul - 7 Sep 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]). The previously inserted 4-call Nov 2025 - Sep 2026 schedule is the Bachelor/single-cycle calendar and was incorrect for this Master.
